message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker — new Markdown link parity issue, bd-3d1t in_progress

- Add bd-3d0j: Markdown parity for OSC8 hyperlinks (links/images should
  use OSC8 sequences not URL suffix text), priority 2 feature
- bd-3d1t (parity audit): open → in_progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3bmm","title":"E2E test: Markup parsing and Console print","description":"End-to-end integration test for markup and Console:
MARKUP TEST:
(1) Parse rich markup strings ([bold red]text[/])
(2) Verify style tag nesting and inheritance
(3) Test color parsing (named, hex, rgb)
(4) Log: tag parsing, style stack, span creation

CONSOLE PRINT TEST:
(1) Console::print() with markup string
(2) Console::print_styled() with Text object
(3) Console::print_renderable() with various renderables
(4) Verify final ANSI output
(5) Log: markup expansion, segment joining, newline handling","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:57.633938796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T19:49:33.672029411Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T19:49:33.671964991Z","close_reason":"Already satisfied: e2e_rendering.rs has 29 tests covering markup parsing, Console print, style stacking, color parsing, span creation. e2e_text.rs has 79 tests. All pass with logging.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bmm","depends_on_id":"bd-1rab","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3c90","title":"Full Python Rich parity (beyond current core)","description":"Owner request: remove/exhaust all parity gaps vs upstream Python Rich (currently 13.9.4 in conformance env).

Track missing modules/features (theme, emoji codes, pretty/inspect, traceback, prompt/input, status/pager, advanced progress, etc), expand conformance fixtures, and update RICH_SPEC/FEATURE_PARITY accordingly.

Definition of done:
- All Python Rich public modules/features have Rust equivalents or documented Rust-idiomatic equivalents
- Conformance coverage expanded to cover new features and matches Python outputs where applicable
- README + docs reflect full parity
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:37:32.006665146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:33:32.463468959Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:33:32.463404759Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3czr","title":"demo_showcase tests: live-mode smoke (forced terminal)","description":"Add an e2e test that runs a short live-mode demo with --force-terminal and verifies clean start/stop (bounded output).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:15:32.595139460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:07:38.733449829Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:07:38.733315068Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","live","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3czr","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3czr","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3czr","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3czr","depends_on_id":"bd-3fph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3czr","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-3czr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Test intent

We can't fully validate “looks great in a real TTY” in CI, but we *can* validate that live mode:
- starts successfully
- runs for a bounded amount of time
- stops cleanly (no panic)

## Approach

- Run with `--force-terminal --live --no-screen --quick --speed <fast>`
- Keep duration bounded (e.g. runner has an internal max tick count in non-interactive contexts, or test passes a `--max-frames` style flag if we add one)
- Assert exit 0 and output is within an upper bound

## Dependencies

This requires the live driver to exist (`bd-3fph`) and the scene runner (`bd-1jk8`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:15:45Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3d1t","title":"Parity audit: enumerate remaining Python Rich 13.9.4 gaps","description":"Systematically compare rich_rust behavior/API vs Python Rich 13.9.4. Deliverables: (1) explicit gap list with file/line references, (2) update FEATURE_PARITY.md to be accurate + non-handwavy, (3) break gaps into actionable Beads issues with dependencies and estimates. This is the umbrella issue for reaching 100% feature-for-feature parity.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:51:19.210664595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:51:19.221212242Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","parity"]}
+{"id":"bd-3d0j","title":"Markdown parity: render links/images as OSC8 hyperlinks (no URL suffix)","description":"Python Rich 13.9.4 Markdown renders links and images as OSC8 hyperlinks (default `hyperlinks=true`) without appending the URL text.

Acceptance criteria:
- For Markdown links: `[text](url)` renders as just `text` in plain output; ANSI output wraps `text` in OSC8 sequences pointing to `url`.
- For Markdown images: `![alt](url)` renders as `🌆 alt` (image emoji + space + alt text) in plain output; ANSI output wraps `alt` in OSC8 pointing to `url`.
- Provide a Rust API equivalent to Python `Markdown(..., hyperlinks: bool)`; when disabled, render without OSC8.
- Add conformance fixtures: `markdown/link`, `markdown/image`.

Notes:
- Current rich_rust behavior appends ` (url)` to link text; remove for parity.
- Reuse existing OSC8 support via `Style::link(...)`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:12:12.702065296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:12:26.546091662Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["links","markdown","parity"]}

Verify all child renderables render correctly within group context.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:51.091371482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:55:36.690155038Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:55:36.690025847Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3egk","depends_on_id":"bd-1gyp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3fe1","title":"Migrate interactive.rs to consistent mutex handling","description":"## Task Description

Migrate mutex lock() calls in `src/interactive.rs` Status struct to use the new sync utility functions.

## Current State Analysis

Status struct has one Mutex-protected field:
```rust
pub struct Status {
    message: Arc<Mutex<String>>,  // Line 30
    live: Option<Live>,
}
```

## Lock Calls to Migrate

| Line | Current Pattern | Context | New Pattern |
|------|-----------------|---------|-------------|
| 40 | `if let Ok(message) = message.lock()` | new() - print fallback | `lock_recover` |
| 67 | `.lock().map(|m| m.clone()).unwrap_or_default()` | new() - render closure | `lock_recover` |
| 83 | `if let Ok(mut slot) = self.message.lock()` | update() | `lock_recover` |

## Migration Pattern

Before (line 83):
```rust
pub fn update(&self, message: impl Into<String>) {
    if let Ok(mut slot) = self.message.lock() {
        *slot = message.into();
    }
    if let Some(live) = &self.live {
        let _ = live.refresh();
    }
}
```

After:
```rust
use crate::sync::lock_recover;

pub fn update(&self, message: impl Into<String>) {
    let mut slot = lock_recover(&self.message);
    *slot = message.into();
    drop(slot);  // Release lock before calling refresh
    if let Some(live) = &self.live {
        let _ = live.refresh();
    }
}
```

## Render Closure Challenge

Line 67 is inside a closure passed to `get_renderable()`:
```rust
let live = Live::with_options(...).get_renderable(move || {
    // ...
    let msg = message_for_render
        .lock()
        .map(|m| m.clone())
        .unwrap_or_default();
    // ...
});
```

This closure needs access to `lock_recover`. Options:
1. Import crate::sync inside the closure (not possible for closures)
2. Use a local helper before the closure
3. Keep as-is since it is unlikely to fail

**Recommendation**: Migrate to:
```rust
let msg = crate::sync::lock_recover(&message_for_render).clone();
```

## Test Code

SharedBuffer in tests uses `.lock().unwrap()` - keep these as-is for test clarity.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-288g (sync module must exist first)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 3 lock() calls in production code migrated
- [ ] Closure access to sync module resolved
- [ ] No behavior changes (pure refactoring)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] cargo clippy passes","notes":"Already migrated - uses crate::sync::lock_recover throughout (5 usages in Status)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:12:23.247356071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:37:58.892764910Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:37:58.892612566Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fe1","depends_on_id":"bd-288g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3fe1","depends_on_id":"bd-88kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ff5","title":"demo_showcase: table showcase scene","description":"Create a table-heavy scene (services/metrics) demonstrating alignment, styles, and status badges.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:09.286125556Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:20.743449798Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:20.743388554Z","close_reason":"TableScene with service status, metrics (numeric alignment), and ASCII fallback demos","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","table"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ff5","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ff5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ff5","depends_on_id":"bd-2175","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ff5","depends_on_id":"bd-2hba","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":50,"issue_id":"bd-3ff5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Table targets

Demonstrate:
- header styling + column justification
- min/max widths + wrapping
- markup cells (status badges)
- numeric alignment

Use realistic data: services, versions, latency, error rate, region, etc.

## Acceptance criteria

- Looks good both wide and narrow (narrow may omit columns).
- Includes at least one ASCII fallback example (or shows how to toggle).
